---
id: "19.2.4"
title: "HCI Workload Placement Recommendation Acceptance and Stale Recommendation Tracking"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.2.4 · HCI Workload Placement Recommendation Acceptance and Stale Recommendation Tracking

## Description

Monitors hyperconverged recommendation pipeline health—generation, acknowledgment, acceptance, execution proxies, stale backlog, affinity and anti-affinity violations, blocked migrations, and automation posture drift—rather than restating steady-state node skew. UC-19.2.8 already measures per-node distribution imbalance used to trigger planners; this control explains whether Distributed Resource Scheduler, Acropolis Dynamic Scheduling, or Azure Stack HCI Resource Balancer governance is actually consuming advice. UC-19.2.16 documents failure-domain posture that constrains legal moves; UC-19.2.27 frames vSAN slack so capacity-related move blocks are read beside free-space reserve policy instead of mis-labeled scheduler faults.

## Value

Ignored placement advice behaves like an invisible configuration change: hotspots persist through patch cycles, migrations stack on the same sockets, and finance sees healthy averages while operators burn time on manual shuffles. Quantifying acceptance, staleness, outstanding rule breaches, blocked moves, and silent automation downgrades gives executives an auditable answer to whether orchestrators are trustworthy before the next maintenance wave or audit asks for proof of automated governance.

## Implementation

Ingest vmware:drs:* and vmware:vc:event with PowerCLI or TA forwarding, nutanix:ads:* plus Splunk_TA_nutanix baselines, and azure_stack_hci:* exporter jobs; normalize time to UTC; publish hci_drs_owner.csv with automation and acceptance targets; define the comment macro; schedule the multisearch SPL every fifteen or thirty minutes; wire severity to owner_team queues and archive monthly evidence exports alongside vendor screenshots.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites

This use case sits on the recommendation lifecycle and automation posture for hyperconverged and software-defined compute clusters. UC-19.2.8 already answers whether steady-state utilization is skewed across nodes; this control asks whether the orchestrator is actually producing, approving, and executing placement advice that closes those gaps. UC-19.2.16 remains the authoritative view of configured failure domains and rack awareness, which constrains what recommendations are even legal. UC-19.2.27 continues to headline vSAN slack and rebuild headroom so capacity-based move blocks are interpreted beside free-space policy, not mistaken for a broken scheduler. Keep cluster_id strings identical across those lookups to avoid split-brain reporting during audits.

Required feeds use vendor-precise sourcetypes so field aliasing stays deterministic after Technical Add-on upgrades. VMware and HyperFlex share the vmware:drs:* and vmware:vc:event arms: map Cisco HyperFlex clusters to the same cluster_id string HyperFlex operators use in vCenter because HX relies on vSphere Distributed Resource Scheduler as the primary mover. Nutanix Acropolis Dynamic Scheduling lands on nutanix:ads:recommendation with optional nutanix:vm:migration and nutanix:ads:vm_affinity for blocked moves and outstanding affinity conflicts. Azure Stack HCI contributes azure_stack_hci:resourcebalancer:state, azure_stack_hci:vm:migration, and azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event from scheduled PowerShell or a universal forwarder script on a privileged orchestration host.

Governance lives in lookups/hci_drs_owner.csv referenced as hci_drs_owner inside transforms.conf. Columns are cluster_id, vendor, owner_team, target_min_acceptance_rate_pct defaulting logically to sixty, target_max_stale_hours defaulting to eight, target_max_violation_count defaulting to zero, expected_automation_level defaulting to Fully, and change_freeze_until as ISO8601 UTC or empty. The Splunk join intentionally uses a subsearch with inputlookup rather than the lookup command so the same pattern works on standalone search heads without accidental automatic lookup configuration mismatches.

Role model: operators need search access to the hci, vmware, nutanix, and microsoft indexes you actually deploy; HEC tokens must be rotated on the same cadence as your secrets vault policy. Document time zone normalization at ingest because recommendation timestamps that arrive in local time skew staleness math. Store sample events for each sourcetype in a private test index when validating the eval chains.

Network access patterns include HTTPS from a collector to Nutanix Prism Central for v3 dynamic scheduler recommendations, Prism Element REST for vm-level affinity and migration history, read-only vCenter APIs for PowerCLI exports, and WinRM-limited jump hosts for Azure Stack HCI resource balancer state. Maintain separate service accounts per environment and avoid logging guest display names if your data classification forbids it.

Volume planning assumes one cluster settings event per fifteen minutes per cluster, dozens of recommendation rows per day on active estates, and bursty migration failure lines during maintenance. Budget at least ninety days of hot retention for acceptance trending and cold storage for annual governance reviews.

Multisearch design keeps VMware, Nutanix, and Azure Stack HCI parsers isolated so a renamed JSON key on one stack does not null out the others. Each arm ends with stats latest grouped by cluster_id, rec_id, and vendor as required by the gold profile for this programme. HyperFlex does not add a fourth arm; operators ingest HyperFlex under the VMware sourcetype mapping with HX cluster names in cluster_id.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

VMware vSphere and HyperFlex: install Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380) to collect vmware:vc:event where your architecture places the forwarder tier. Add scheduled PowerCLI exports from a hardened automation host that authenticates with least privilege and posts JSON lines per recommendation to sourcetype vmware:drs:recommendation using fields such as cluster_id, recommendation_id, status, created_epoch, accepted_epoch, and blocked_reason when the export includes it. Emit cluster automation posture from Get-DrsCluster to vmware:drs:cluster_settings with drs_automation_level or automation_level populated in plain text and rec_id forced to __cluster__ at ingest so downstream searches can lift automation_level_current without colliding with guest rec ids. Emit affinity and anti-affinity violations from Get-DrsRule or equivalent REST-derived lists into vmware:drs:rule_violation with violation_id, violation_status, and cluster_id. Optional ucsm:event can correlate UCS blade evacuations when HyperFlex maintenance overlaps Fabric Interconnect work; tag ucs_cluster_name consistently if joined later.

Nutanix: deploy Splunk_TA_nutanix (5076) for baseline Prism inventory telemetry even when custom pollers carry ADS-specific payloads. Implement a small Python or PowerShell poller authorized to Prism Central GET /api/nutanix/v3/dynamicscheduler/recommendations every five to fifteen minutes, flattening each recommendation to one HEC event on nutanix:ads:recommendation with cluster_id, recommendation_id, lifecycle timestamps, and human-readable blocking reasons when the API exposes them. Poll Prism Element for vm host_affinity endpoints into nutanix:ads:vm_affinity when affinity violations must reflect live intent versus current host, and ingest migration attempts to nutanix:vm:migration with success booleans and error_code fields that map to blocked migrations in Splunk.

Azure Stack HCI: schedule PowerShell on a management cluster node or jump host every ten minutes to export Get-ClusterParameter resource balancer-related state into azure_stack_hci:resourcebalancer:state with cluster_id, automation_level or resource_balancer_mode text, and counters your team standardizes. Capture Move-VM history and failures into azure_stack_hci:vm:migration with error categories for rule conflict, host capacity, and network bandwidth when Windows events or cluster logs expose them. Ship Get-ClusterLog excerpts or summarized load balance events to azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event with concise summaries so volume stays bounded; compress multiline payloads at the collector before HEC.

Ingest hygiene: set TRUNCATE high enough for JSON exports, enforce INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json on indexers parsing HEC JSON, and use a dedicated source naming convention per collector script. Time keys must be UTC; if a vendor emits epoch seconds, index them as opaque fields and let Splunk use _time from arrival only when clock skew is worse than five minutes. Add a Technical Add-on sidecar app TA_hci_drs_governance holding props.conf FIELDALIAS stanzas when a vendor renames keys between quarters.

props.conf guidance: each sourcetype should set SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false, LINE_BREAKER for newline-delimited JSON, and TIME_PREFIX if the JSON time field is stable. transforms.conf should not silently drop recommendation records when cluster_id is missing; instead route those lines to a dead letter index for operator triage.

Security: protect lookup files containing owner_team emails behind role ACLs, store automation credentials in the OS secret store the forwarder can read, and forbid interactive administrator sessions on the poller host. Enable TLS on all HEC endpoints and separate tokens per vendor to narrow blast radius.

Capacity side notes: acceptance and stale metrics depend on continuous recommendation traffic; if a collector is offline longer than one poll interval, expect artificial dips that clear when backlog posts—document that behavior in the runbook rather than chasing phantom automation drift.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Operationalize the saved search hci_uc_19_2_4_rec_pipeline with a fifteen- or thirty-minute cron over earliest=-25h@h latest=@h so twenty-four hour recommendation math aligns with wall-clock buckets. Keep the macro comment wrapper so dispatch logs identify UC-19.2.4 during audits.

How this search behaves: multisearch fans out three vendor arms that each normalize snake_case and camelCase keys with coalesce, collapse multiple telemetry lines per recommendation with stats latest by cluster_id rec_id vendor, join governance targets from hci_drs_owner using inputlookup inside a left join, compute stale recommendations by comparing now against gen_ts when acc_ts is null, aggregate per cluster to recommendations_generated_24h and recommendations_accepted_24h, derive acceptance_rate_pct with a neutral default when no recommendations arrived, extract automation_level_current primarily from __cluster__ rows carried through eventstats, compute automation_level_drift_indicator against expected_automation_level, add streamstats for a short rolling acceptance trend, apply the severity ladder with automation drift plus acceptance and staleness thresholds, demote to info during change_freeze_until windows, and project the routing table columns.

Alert routing: critical should page platform engineering and storage leadership in parallel when automation silently drops toward manual while recommendations stagnate or violations persist. High should open a Sev-2 ticket with vendor packet captures. Warning can feed email and chat. Info still renders on dashboards for trend visibility.

Throttle guidance: suppress duplicate critical alerts on cluster_id for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates from warning to critical within the same two intervals.

Save-as-alert checklist: confirm macro exists, confirm role index permissions, confirm lookup published, attach runbook link, and store a PNG of the results table quarterly in the evidence repository.

Fenced SPL for runbooks (must match the spl JSON field aside from index renames your change board approves):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.4 HCI Workload Placement Recommendation Acceptance and Stale Recommendation Tracking. Tunables: hci_drs_owner.csv (target_min_acceptance_rate_pct default 60, target_max_stale_hours default 8, target_max_violation_count default 0, expected_automation_level default Fully). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until demotes severity to info. Cisco HyperFlex shares the VMware DRS sourcetype mapping; set cluster_id to the HX cluster name in vCenter. Replace index=hci with indexes approved for your SO.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:drs:recommendation" OR sourcetype="vmware:drs:cluster_settings" OR sourcetype="vmware:drs:rule_violation") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, dest_cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval rec_id=if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:cluster_settings", "__cluster__", toString(coalesce(recommendation_id, recommendationId, rec_id, recId, recommendation_key, violation_id, rule_violation_id, md5(cluster_id."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval vendor="vmware"
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(action, event_type, EventType, operation, op, opType, type, "")))
      | eval st=lower(toString(coalesce(status, recommendation_status, rec_state, recommendationState, violation_status, state, "")))
      | eval gen_ts=coalesce(tonumber(created_epoch), tonumber(generated_epoch), tonumber(create_epoch), if(match(st,"pending|proposed|new|open|generated|waiting"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"recommend|proposal|generate|create|pending"), _time, null()), if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:recommendation", _time, null()))
      | eval acc_ts=coalesce(tonumber(accepted_epoch), tonumber(executed_epoch), tonumber(applied_epoch), if(match(st,"accepted|applied|executed|completed|succeeded"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"accept|apply|execute|approve|commit"), _time, null()))
      | eval violation_flag=if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:rule_violation" AND match(st,"active|open|outstanding|violated|breach|true|error"), 1, if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:rule_violation", 1, 0))
      | eval blocked_flag=if((match(op,"migrate|move|relocate|vmotion|drs") AND match(st,"fail|block|error|cancel|denied")) OR tonumber(tostring(coalesce(blocked, migration_blocked, "0")), 10)=1 OR match(lower(toString(coalesce(block_reason, failure_reason, ""))), "rule|capacity|bandwidth|network|nic|constraint"), 1, 0)
      | eval automation_level_snapshot=case(match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "full|automated"), "Fully", match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "partial|semi"), "Partially", match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "manual"), "Manual", match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "disabl|off|stop"), "Disabled", true(), null())
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(gen_ts) AS gen_ts latest(acc_ts) AS acc_ts latest(st) AS rec_state max(violation_flag) AS violation_flag max(blocked_flag) AS blocked_flag latest(automation_level_snapshot) AS automation_level_snapshot BY cluster_id rec_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:ads:recommendation" OR sourcetype="nutanix:vm:migration" OR sourcetype="nutanix:ads:vm_affinity") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval rec_id=toString(coalesce(recommendation_id, recommendationId, recommendation_uuid, migration_uuid, migration_id, uuid, violation_id, affinity_entity_key, md5(cluster_id."|".tostring(_time))))
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(action, operation, event_type, op, "")))
      | eval st=lower(toString(coalesce(status, recommendation_status, state, migration_status, violation_status, affinity_state, "")))
      | eval gen_ts=coalesce(tonumber(created_epoch), tonumber(generated_epoch), if(sourcetype="nutanix:ads:recommendation", _time, null()), if(match(st,"pending|proposed|new|open|waiting"), _time, null()))
      | eval acc_ts=coalesce(tonumber(accepted_epoch), tonumber(executed_epoch), if(match(st,"accepted|applied|executed|completed|succeeded|done"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"accept|apply|execute"), _time, null()))
      | eval violation_flag=if(sourcetype="nutanix:ads:vm_affinity" AND match(st,"violated|breach|invalid|conflict|outstanding|open|active"), 1, if(sourcetype="nutanix:ads:vm_affinity" AND tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_violation, violation_flag_field, "0")), 10)=1, 1, 0))
      | eval blocked_flag=if(sourcetype="nutanix:vm:migration" AND (match(st,"fail|block|error|cancel|abort|denied") OR tonumber(tostring(coalesce(success, "1")), 10)=0), 1, 0)
      | eval automation_level_snapshot=case(match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "full|automated"), "Fully", match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "partial"), "Partially", match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "manual"), "Manual", match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "disabl"), "Disabled", true(), null())
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(gen_ts) AS gen_ts latest(acc_ts) AS acc_ts latest(st) AS rec_state max(violation_flag) AS violation_flag max(blocked_flag) AS blocked_flag latest(automation_level_snapshot) AS automation_level_snapshot BY cluster_id rec_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:resourcebalancer:state" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vm:migration" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, failover_cluster_name, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval rec_id=if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:resourcebalancer:state", "__cluster__", toString(coalesce(recommendation_id, migration_id, event_id, uuid, md5(cluster_id."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(action, operation, event_type, op, "")))
      | eval st=lower(toString(coalesce(status, migration_status, state, outcome, "")))
      | eval gen_ts=coalesce(tonumber(created_epoch), if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event" AND match(op,"recommend|proposal|generate"), _time, null()), if(match(st,"pending|proposed|new|open"), _time, null()))
      | eval acc_ts=coalesce(tonumber(accepted_epoch), tonumber(executed_epoch), if(match(st,"accepted|completed|succeeded|applied"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"accept|apply|execute"), _time, null()))
      | eval violation_flag=if(match(st,"affinity|anti|rule|violation|constraint") AND match(st,"fail|violation|breach|block|conflict"), 1, 0)
      | eval blocked_flag=if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vm:migration" AND (match(st,"fail|block|error|cancel|abort|denied") OR tonumber(tostring(coalesce(success, "1")), 10)=0), 1, if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event" AND match(lower(toString(coalesce(reason, detail, ""))), "rule|capacity|bandwidth|network|blocked"), 1, 0))
      | eval automation_level_snapshot=case(match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "full|auto"), "Fully", match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "partial|semi"), "Partially", match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "manual|idle"), "Manual", match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "disabl|off|stop"), "Disabled", true(), null())
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(gen_ts) AS gen_ts latest(acc_ts) AS acc_ts latest(st) AS rec_state max(violation_flag) AS violation_flag max(blocked_flag) AS blocked_flag latest(automation_level_snapshot) AS automation_level_snapshot BY cluster_id rec_id vendor ]
| where isnotnull(cluster_id) AND cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_drs_owner
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_min_acceptance_rate_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct, "60")), 10)
     | eval target_max_stale_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_stale_hours, "8")), 10)
     | eval target_max_violation_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_violation_count, "0")), 10)
     | eval expected_automation_level=toString(coalesce(expected_automation_level, "Fully"))
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_min_acceptance_rate_pct target_max_stale_hours target_max_violation_count expected_automation_level change_freeze_until ]
| eval tgt_stale=target_max_stale_hours
| eventstats latest(eval(if(rec_id=="__cluster__", automation_level_snapshot, null()))) AS cluster_automation BY cluster_id vendor
| eval automation_level_snapshot=coalesce(automation_level_snapshot, cluster_automation)
| fillnull value=0 violation_flag blocked_flag
| eval gen_in_24=if((!match(rec_id, "^__cluster__$")) AND isnotnull(gen_ts) AND gen_ts>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h"), 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 gen_in_24
| eval acc_in_24=if((!match(rec_id, "^__cluster__$")) AND isnotnull(acc_ts) AND acc_ts>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h"), 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 acc_in_24
| eval blocked_24h=if((blocked_flag=1) AND (_time>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h")), 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 blocked_24h
| eval is_stale=if((!match(rec_id, "^__cluster__$")) AND isnotnull(gen_ts) AND isnull(acc_ts) AND ((now()-gen_ts)>(tgt_stale*3600)), 1, 0)
| eval stale_hours=if(is_stale=1, round(((now()-gen_ts)/3600.0), 4), null())
| eventstats avg(gen_ts) AS cluster_context_avg_gen_ts BY cluster_id vendor
| stats sum(gen_in_24) AS recommendations_generated_24h sum(acc_in_24) AS recommendations_accepted_24h sum(is_stale) AS recommendations_stale_count max(stale_hours) AS oldest_stale_hours sum(violation_flag) AS affinity_violation_count sum(blocked_24h) AS migrations_blocked_24h latest(cluster_automation) AS automation_level_current latest(owner_team) AS owner_team latest(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct) AS target_min_acceptance_rate_pct latest(target_max_stale_hours) AS target_max_stale_hours latest(target_max_violation_count) AS target_max_violation_count latest(expected_automation_level) AS expected_automation_level latest(change_freeze_until) AS change_freeze_until BY cluster_id vendor
| eval target_min_acceptance_rate_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct, "60")), 10)
| eval target_max_stale_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_stale_hours, "8")), 10)
| eval target_max_violation_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_violation_count, "0")), 10)
| eval expected_automation_level=coalesce(expected_automation_level, "Fully")
| fillnull value=0 recommendations_generated_24h recommendations_accepted_24h recommendations_stale_count affinity_violation_count migrations_blocked_24h
| eval acceptance_rate_pct=if((recommendations_generated_24h>0), round((100.0*recommendations_accepted_24h/recommendations_generated_24h), 2), if((recommendations_generated_24h=0) AND (recommendations_accepted_24h=0), 100, null()))
| fillnull value=100 acceptance_rate_pct
| eval acceptance_rate_pct=if(acceptance_rate_pct>100, 100, acceptance_rate_pct)
| eval automation_level_current=case(isnull(automation_level_current) OR (len(toString(automation_level_current))=0), "Unknown", 1=1, automation_level_current)
| eval automation_level_drift_indicator=if(lower(toString(automation_level_current))!=lower(toString(expected_automation_level)), 1, 0)
| eval oldest_stale_hours=coalesce(oldest_stale_hours, 0)
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t first(acceptance_rate_pct) AS prior_acceptance_rate_pct BY cluster_id vendor
| eval sev0=case((automation_level_drift_indicator=1) AND ((acceptance_rate_pct<25) OR (oldest_stale_hours>8) OR (affinity_violation_count>0)), "critical", (acceptance_rate_pct<target_min_acceptance_rate_pct), "high", (oldest_stale_hours>target_max_stale_hours), "high", (affinity_violation_count>target_max_violation_count), "high", (acceptance_rate_pct<(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct+15)), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND isnotnull(ft) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| table vendor cluster_id acceptance_rate_pct recommendations_generated_24h recommendations_stale_count oldest_stale_hours affinity_violation_count migrations_blocked_24h automation_level_current severity owner_team change_freeze_until
```

Step 3 — Validate

Field spot checks: run each multisearch arm alone with a fifteen-minute window, confirm cluster_id and rec_id appear on every row except purposeful drops, and compare counts of vmware:drs:recommendation events in Splunk to the vCenter Distributed Resource Scheduler recommendation list for the same interval. Validate automation_level_current by opening cluster settings in the vendor UI while a __cluster__ event is indexing. For Nutanix, compare Acropolis Dynamic Scheduling backlog to nutanix:ads:recommendation volume. For Azure Stack HCI, compare Move-VM failure history in Windows Admin Center to azure_stack_hci:vm:migration.

Synthetic stale test: in lab, ingest a recommendation with created_epoch twenty hours in the past and no accepted_epoch while target_max_stale_hours is eight; expect recommendations_stale_count to increment and oldest_stale_hours to exceed eight after join math.

Acceptance math test: send five generated and two accepted pairs inside twenty-four hours; acceptance_rate_pct should approximate forty within rounding tolerance. Zero-generation windows should land near the neutral default without dividing by zero.

Governance join test: remove a cluster_id from hci_drs_owner temporarily and confirm defaults still populate targets instead of nulling the pipeline; restore the row after validation.

Cross-UC coherence: when UC-19.2.8 shows skew but UC-19.2.4 shows healthy acceptance, suspect deliberate human deferral rather than automation failure; conversely, healthy balance with collapsing acceptance hints at change freezes or policy blocks worth reading beside UC-19.2.16 placement rules.

Performance test: schedule during business hours, capture Job Inspector scan counts, and tune post-filter as needed if a noisy vmware:vc:event stream dominates; consider adding indexed index-time filters for drs in props when appropriate.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard layout: trellis vendor with single value acceptance_rate_pct, a timechart of recommendations_generated_24h versus recommendations_accepted_24h from a summary index populated hourly, a table identical to the alert projection, and a secondary panel for migrations_blocked_24h stacked by error category when error_code exists in ingest.

Runbook cadence: daily ownership review of clusters with acceptance below target_min_acceptance_rate_pct, weekly stale backlog grooming, monthly automation posture audit comparing expected_automation_level to automation_level_current, and quarterly evidence export to the restricted archive.

Integration hooks: push severity to the ticketing system with owner_team in the assignment field, attach UC-19.2.8 deep links when skew persists two intervals, and attach UC-19.2.27 charts when blocked migrations mention disk space or component rebuild constraints.

Training: walk operators through distinguishing policy-induced backlog from true telemetry loss using collector heartbeat dashboards outside Splunk; that distinction prevents mis-triaged outages.

Documentation: store collector source code in git with checksum tagged to the Splunk app revision table; tie recommendation field dictionaries to the same commit hash as the SPL for audit defensibility.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Case 1 Lookup join misses:** Rows show blank owner_team and default targets never appear. Confirm transforms.conf stanza hci_drs_owner exists, confirm CSV column names match exactly including underscores, confirm cluster_id spelling matches between vendors and the lookup, and re-run inputlookup hci_drs_owner in Search to validate CSV reload.

• **Case 2 Zero recommendations_generated_24h despite heavy UI backlog:** Timestamp fields may not map into gen_ts; extend coalesce lists in the affected arm, confirm _time versus vendor event time, and verify sourcetype typos in inputs.conf.

• **Case 3 acceptance_rate_pct pegged at 100 with visible skew:** Acceptance timestamps may populate incorrectly; inspect acc_ts derivations and ensure accepted actions are not double-counted across vmware:vc:event and vmware:drs:recommendation; dedupe at ingest if both streams repeat the same transition.

• **Case 4 automation_level_current stuck Unknown:** Missing __cluster__ settings rows; validate vmware:drs:cluster_settings and azure_stack_hci:resourcebalancer:state collectors, extend automation_mode coalesce aliases, and confirm rec_id equals __cluster__ for those sourcetypes.

• **Case 5 migrations_blocked_24h always zero:** blocked_flag patterns may not match your error text; broaden match() clauses or capture a normalized error_class at ingest; confirm _time on migration events falls inside the search window.

• **Case 6 affinity_violation_count noisy during maintenance:** Violation exports may stay open while hosts drain; cross-reference maintenance calendars, set temporary change_freeze_until, or enrich with host maintenance flags via a secondary join.

• **Case 7 streamstats trend oscillates wildly on sparse clusters:** Low event volume makes window=5 unstable; increase window or disable streamstats for those clusters via a macro parameter in an extended fork documented in the same repo.

• **Case 8 Severity stuck at info during incidents:** change_freeze_until may still be active; parse freeze timestamps in UTC, confirm strptime format coverage for your ISO variant, and avoid leaving eternal freeze sentinels in the CSV after maintenance completes.

• **Case 9 Nutanix ADS API pagination drops recommendations:** Poller may only read the first page; implement cursor-aware retrieval and log page counts to a side metric for Splunk-side reconciliation.

• **Case 10 PowerCLI credential rotation halts exports:** Service account lockouts produce silent gaps; add heartbeat sourcetype lines from the automation host and alert on absence independent of recommendation volume.

Closing checklist: multisearch arms return rows in lab for each vendor within seven days of inputs.conf changes, lookup defaults match finance-approved governance baselines, fieldsummary output is archived monthly, stakeholder runbooks cite this UC id in their titles, and the comment macro registers UC-19.2.4 in internal dispatch auditing feeds.

Supplemental engineering notes continue for version pinning: Splunk_TA_nutanix upgrades sometimes rename Prism keys—diff default transforms before monthly rollout. VMware Add-on upgrades can shift vmware:vc:event field names—track release notes and extend FIELDALIAS before production merge. Azure Stack HCI cumulative updates may change cluster log format—snapshot three sample lines per month in git. Cisco HyperFlex HX Connect API variants may require different JSON paths—version pin in collector config. Security scans should exclude credential files on collector hosts but include auditable checksums of scripts. Capacity planners should export acceptance monthly even when severity stays info. FinOps should correlate blocked migrations with overcommitted CPU packs. Application teams should annotate deliberate anti-affinity that legitimately lowers acceptance. Network teams should treat bandwidth-blocked migrations as NIC or QoS tickets, not storage tickets. Identity teams should ensure service account MFA exemptions follow policy. Legal should review whether guest names appear in exports. Internal audit should demand screenshots of vendor consoles beside Splunk rows for critical findings. Disaster recovery should replicate lookup CSVs with the same RPO as other knowledge objects. Tabletop exercises should practice rollback of automation level changes. Training modules should quiz engineers on stale versus pending semantics. Documentation portals should cross-link to this JSON as the authoritative SPL source. Continuous improvement should track mean time to clear stale recommendations as a leadership KPI.



## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.4 HCI Workload Placement Recommendation Acceptance and Stale Recommendation Tracking. Tunables: hci_drs_owner.csv (target_min_acceptance_rate_pct default 60, target_max_stale_hours default 8, target_max_violation_count default 0, expected_automation_level default Fully). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until demotes severity to info. Cisco HyperFlex shares the VMware DRS sourcetype mapping; set cluster_id to the HX cluster name in vCenter. Replace index=hci with indexes approved for your SO.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:drs:recommendation" OR sourcetype="vmware:drs:cluster_settings" OR sourcetype="vmware:drs:rule_violation") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, dest_cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval rec_id=if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:cluster_settings", "__cluster__", toString(coalesce(recommendation_id, recommendationId, rec_id, recId, recommendation_key, violation_id, rule_violation_id, md5(cluster_id."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval vendor="vmware"
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(action, event_type, EventType, operation, op, opType, type, "")))
      | eval st=lower(toString(coalesce(status, recommendation_status, rec_state, recommendationState, violation_status, state, "")))
      | eval gen_ts=coalesce(tonumber(created_epoch), tonumber(generated_epoch), tonumber(create_epoch), if(match(st,"pending|proposed|new|open|generated|waiting"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"recommend|proposal|generate|create|pending"), _time, null()), if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:recommendation", _time, null()))
      | eval acc_ts=coalesce(tonumber(accepted_epoch), tonumber(executed_epoch), tonumber(applied_epoch), if(match(st,"accepted|applied|executed|completed|succeeded"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"accept|apply|execute|approve|commit"), _time, null()))
      | eval violation_flag=if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:rule_violation" AND match(st,"active|open|outstanding|violated|breach|true|error"), 1, if(sourcetype="vmware:drs:rule_violation", 1, 0))
      | eval blocked_flag=if((match(op,"migrate|move|relocate|vmotion|drs") AND match(st,"fail|block|error|cancel|denied")) OR tonumber(tostring(coalesce(blocked, migration_blocked, "0")), 10)=1 OR match(lower(toString(coalesce(block_reason, failure_reason, ""))), "rule|capacity|bandwidth|network|nic|constraint"), 1, 0)
      | eval automation_level_snapshot=case(match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "full|automated"), "Fully", match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "partial|semi"), "Partially", match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "manual"), "Manual", match(lower(toString(coalesce(drs_automation_level, automation_level, automationMode, drsMode, ""))), "disabl|off|stop"), "Disabled", true(), null())
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(gen_ts) AS gen_ts latest(acc_ts) AS acc_ts latest(st) AS rec_state max(violation_flag) AS violation_flag max(blocked_flag) AS blocked_flag latest(automation_level_snapshot) AS automation_level_snapshot BY cluster_id rec_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:ads:recommendation" OR sourcetype="nutanix:vm:migration" OR sourcetype="nutanix:ads:vm_affinity") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_uuid, clusterUuid, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval rec_id=toString(coalesce(recommendation_id, recommendationId, recommendation_uuid, migration_uuid, migration_id, uuid, violation_id, affinity_entity_key, md5(cluster_id."|".tostring(_time))))
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(action, operation, event_type, op, "")))
      | eval st=lower(toString(coalesce(status, recommendation_status, state, migration_status, violation_status, affinity_state, "")))
      | eval gen_ts=coalesce(tonumber(created_epoch), tonumber(generated_epoch), if(sourcetype="nutanix:ads:recommendation", _time, null()), if(match(st,"pending|proposed|new|open|waiting"), _time, null()))
      | eval acc_ts=coalesce(tonumber(accepted_epoch), tonumber(executed_epoch), if(match(st,"accepted|applied|executed|completed|succeeded|done"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"accept|apply|execute"), _time, null()))
      | eval violation_flag=if(sourcetype="nutanix:ads:vm_affinity" AND match(st,"violated|breach|invalid|conflict|outstanding|open|active"), 1, if(sourcetype="nutanix:ads:vm_affinity" AND tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_violation, violation_flag_field, "0")), 10)=1, 1, 0))
      | eval blocked_flag=if(sourcetype="nutanix:vm:migration" AND (match(st,"fail|block|error|cancel|abort|denied") OR tonumber(tostring(coalesce(success, "1")), 10)=0), 1, 0)
      | eval automation_level_snapshot=case(match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "full|automated"), "Fully", match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "partial"), "Partially", match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "manual"), "Manual", match(lower(toString(coalesce(ads_automation_level, automation_level, ""))), "disabl"), "Disabled", true(), null())
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(gen_ts) AS gen_ts latest(acc_ts) AS acc_ts latest(st) AS rec_state max(violation_flag) AS violation_flag max(blocked_flag) AS blocked_flag latest(automation_level_snapshot) AS automation_level_snapshot BY cluster_id rec_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:resourcebalancer:state" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vm:migration" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, failover_cluster_name, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval rec_id=if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:resourcebalancer:state", "__cluster__", toString(coalesce(recommendation_id, migration_id, event_id, uuid, md5(cluster_id."|".tostring(_time)))))
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | eval op=lower(toString(coalesce(action, operation, event_type, op, "")))
      | eval st=lower(toString(coalesce(status, migration_status, state, outcome, "")))
      | eval gen_ts=coalesce(tonumber(created_epoch), if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event" AND match(op,"recommend|proposal|generate"), _time, null()), if(match(st,"pending|proposed|new|open"), _time, null()))
      | eval acc_ts=coalesce(tonumber(accepted_epoch), tonumber(executed_epoch), if(match(st,"accepted|completed|succeeded|applied"), _time, null()), if(match(op,"accept|apply|execute"), _time, null()))
      | eval violation_flag=if(match(st,"affinity|anti|rule|violation|constraint") AND match(st,"fail|violation|breach|block|conflict"), 1, 0)
      | eval blocked_flag=if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vm:migration" AND (match(st,"fail|block|error|cancel|abort|denied") OR tonumber(tostring(coalesce(success, "1")), 10)=0), 1, if(sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:loadbalance:event" AND match(lower(toString(coalesce(reason, detail, ""))), "rule|capacity|bandwidth|network|blocked"), 1, 0))
      | eval automation_level_snapshot=case(match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "full|auto"), "Fully", match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "partial|semi"), "Partially", match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "manual|idle"), "Manual", match(lower(toString(coalesce(resource_balancer_mode, automation_level, cluster_automation, ""))), "disabl|off|stop"), "Disabled", true(), null())
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(gen_ts) AS gen_ts latest(acc_ts) AS acc_ts latest(st) AS rec_state max(violation_flag) AS violation_flag max(blocked_flag) AS blocked_flag latest(automation_level_snapshot) AS automation_level_snapshot BY cluster_id rec_id vendor ]
| where isnotnull(cluster_id) AND cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_drs_owner
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_min_acceptance_rate_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct, "60")), 10)
     | eval target_max_stale_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_stale_hours, "8")), 10)
     | eval target_max_violation_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_violation_count, "0")), 10)
     | eval expected_automation_level=toString(coalesce(expected_automation_level, "Fully"))
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_min_acceptance_rate_pct target_max_stale_hours target_max_violation_count expected_automation_level change_freeze_until ]
| eval tgt_stale=target_max_stale_hours
| eventstats latest(eval(if(rec_id=="__cluster__", automation_level_snapshot, null()))) AS cluster_automation BY cluster_id vendor
| eval automation_level_snapshot=coalesce(automation_level_snapshot, cluster_automation)
| fillnull value=0 violation_flag blocked_flag
| eval gen_in_24=if((!match(rec_id, "^__cluster__$")) AND isnotnull(gen_ts) AND gen_ts>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h"), 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 gen_in_24
| eval acc_in_24=if((!match(rec_id, "^__cluster__$")) AND isnotnull(acc_ts) AND acc_ts>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h"), 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 acc_in_24
| eval blocked_24h=if((blocked_flag=1) AND (_time>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h")), 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 blocked_24h
| eval is_stale=if((!match(rec_id, "^__cluster__$")) AND isnotnull(gen_ts) AND isnull(acc_ts) AND ((now()-gen_ts)>(tgt_stale*3600)), 1, 0)
| eval stale_hours=if(is_stale=1, round(((now()-gen_ts)/3600.0), 4), null())
| eventstats avg(gen_ts) AS cluster_context_avg_gen_ts BY cluster_id vendor
| stats sum(gen_in_24) AS recommendations_generated_24h sum(acc_in_24) AS recommendations_accepted_24h sum(is_stale) AS recommendations_stale_count max(stale_hours) AS oldest_stale_hours sum(violation_flag) AS affinity_violation_count sum(blocked_24h) AS migrations_blocked_24h latest(cluster_automation) AS automation_level_current latest(owner_team) AS owner_team latest(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct) AS target_min_acceptance_rate_pct latest(target_max_stale_hours) AS target_max_stale_hours latest(target_max_violation_count) AS target_max_violation_count latest(expected_automation_level) AS expected_automation_level latest(change_freeze_until) AS change_freeze_until BY cluster_id vendor
| eval target_min_acceptance_rate_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct, "60")), 10)
| eval target_max_stale_hours=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_stale_hours, "8")), 10)
| eval target_max_violation_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_violation_count, "0")), 10)
| eval expected_automation_level=coalesce(expected_automation_level, "Fully")
| fillnull value=0 recommendations_generated_24h recommendations_accepted_24h recommendations_stale_count affinity_violation_count migrations_blocked_24h
| eval acceptance_rate_pct=if((recommendations_generated_24h>0), round((100.0*recommendations_accepted_24h/recommendations_generated_24h), 2), if((recommendations_generated_24h=0) AND (recommendations_accepted_24h=0), 100, null()))
| fillnull value=100 acceptance_rate_pct
| eval acceptance_rate_pct=if(acceptance_rate_pct>100, 100, acceptance_rate_pct)
| eval automation_level_current=case(isnull(automation_level_current) OR (len(toString(automation_level_current))=0), "Unknown", 1=1, automation_level_current)
| eval automation_level_drift_indicator=if(lower(toString(automation_level_current))!=lower(toString(expected_automation_level)), 1, 0)
| eval oldest_stale_hours=coalesce(oldest_stale_hours, 0)
| streamstats window=5 global=f current=t first(acceptance_rate_pct) AS prior_acceptance_rate_pct BY cluster_id vendor
| eval sev0=case((automation_level_drift_indicator=1) AND ((acceptance_rate_pct<25) OR (oldest_stale_hours>8) OR (affinity_violation_count>0)), "critical", (acceptance_rate_pct<target_min_acceptance_rate_pct), "high", (oldest_stale_hours>target_max_stale_hours), "high", (affinity_violation_count>target_max_violation_count), "high", (acceptance_rate_pct<(target_min_acceptance_rate_pct+15)), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND isnotnull(ft) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| table vendor cluster_id acceptance_rate_pct recommendations_generated_24h recommendations_stale_count oldest_stale_hours affinity_violation_count migrations_blocked_24h automation_level_current severity owner_team change_freeze_until
```

## Visualization

Trellised single value tiles for acceptance_rate_pct by vendor, paired bar chart of recommendations_generated_24h against notional accepted volume from summary indexing, heat of oldest_stale_hours, alert table matching the closing projection, optional sparkline of prior_acceptance_rate_pct when streamstats output lands in summaries.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere (Splunkbase 5380)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)
- [Splunk Add-on for Nutanix (Splunkbase 5076)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)
- [VMware vSphere — Managing VMware DRS in a cluster](https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-vSphere/8.0/vsphere-resource-management/GUID-10278612-6850-452C-818A-CC61303A6D2E.html)
